rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Costesting First, Last And Always Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO's Mtn Costesting First, Last And Always Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement The motion to contest is denied. American Terrazzo Co. fails to demonstrate that the settlement is "out of the ballpark" within the meaning of Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. See CCP 877.6(d). The settlors have provided substantial evidence that the settlement is in the ballpark. It was not necessary for First, Last & Always to allocate settlement funds toward Paul Ryan's defense costs because First, Last & Always was already providing a defense to Paul Ryan. This order will not determine the amount of any credit to which American Terrazzo would be entitled against any verdict for defense costs that might ultimately be awarded against it. rulings.Case Title: PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. WHITE SNOW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend, as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. Sustained, without leave to amend as to the first, second and third causes of action. For the first and third causes of action, the claims are predicated on a contract between Prince of Peace Enterprises, Inc. and White Snow Corporation ("WSC"). Mr. Eng is not personally a party to the contract and does not have standing to sue on this contract merely by virtue of his capacity as an owner or officer. (Hatchwell v. Blue Shield of California (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 1027, 1034.) For the second cause of action, Mr. Eng has not alleged any facts to show that he, as opposed to WSC was "a person who has suffered injury in fact and has lost money or property as a result of unfair competition." (Bus. & Prof. Code § 17204.) For the fourth cause of action, Mr. Eng has not pleaded that he personally, as opposed to WSC, suffered any damage from the alleged misrepresentation concerning the pricing structure for sale of goods to WSC. For the fifth cause of action, Mr. Eng has not pled any of the elements to support a claim for breach of the implied warranty of merchantability with respect to him personally. (CACI no. 3210.) rulings.Case Title: VERONICA MARIONA VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 6, PETITIONER VERONICA MARIONA MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE Granted. Upon the Court's independent review of the Administrative Record, the weight of the evidence does not support the Administrative Law Judge's finding that Petitioner was not disabled for the performance of her duties at the time of her retirement. (See CCP § 1094.5) Petitioner has met her burden of showing that she was incapacitated because of her disability, as she was substantially unable to perform the usual duties of her Certified Nursing Assistant position. (See Administrative Record 32-50, 117-134, 136-139, 140-145; see also Mansperger v. Public Employees' Retirement System (1970) 6 Cal.App.3d 873.) Dr. Jacob was clear that Petitioner could not go back to work. Both Dr. Lipton and Dr. Missirian qualified their opinions by stating that Petitioner could go back to work subject to restrictions on her ability to perform various duties. The only evidence concerning "usual duties" came from Petitioner's testimony, and the various restrictions on lifting, bending, pushing and so forth fundamentally affect those usual duties. This point is confirmed by the September 23, 2011 letter from the employer stating that it did not have available work within her work restrictions when she applied for medically appropriate work. (Administrative Record 53.) Although Dr. Missirian suggested a belief that Petitioner might someday regain full range of motion, that appears to be based on some doubts about the course of her treatment and her commitment to it, issues as to which Dr. Missirian had no information-as reflected in his "wonder[ing] if she had appropriate" care. Petitioner's physical therapy records are not in the Administrative Record nor is there evidence that Petitioner failed to participate fully in prescribed physical therapy. As such, there is no evidence that Petitioner refused or failed to undergo reasonable treatment that would have allowed her to return to work. (See Reynolds v. City of San Carlos (1981) 126 Cal.App.3d 208.) "All pages of each document and exhibit must be attached together at the top by a method that permits pages to be easily turned and the entire content of each page to be read." (CRC 3.1110(e).) "Each exhibit must be separated by a hard 8 ½ x 11 sheet with hard paper or plastic tabs extending below the bottom of the page, bearing the exhibit designation." (CRC 3.1110(f).) Petitioner may pick up from Department 302 the administrative record lodged on October 21, 2013 and re-lodge it in compliance with CRC 3.1110(c), (e) and (f). rulings.Case Title: ILEANA A. TORUNO VS. BLUEPRINT STUDIOS TRENDS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 1. DEFENDANT BLUEPRINT STUDIOS TRENDS INC.'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Blueprint's motion is denied in part and granted in part. The phone records demanded are electronically stored information (ESI) which are not reasonably accessible because of undue burden or expense. CCP 2031.060(c); See, Weil & Brown, Cal Civ Proc Before Trial 8:1456.21 et seq; 8:1497.5. Blueprint is entitled to see the phone records, but Toruno is not obligated to pay the past due phone bill in order to give Blueprint access. The parties are ordered to meet and confer by January 21, 2014 in order to determine the most efficient and least burdensome and expensive way to access the phone records. One solution may be a subpoena to T-Mobile by Blueprint which Toruno will cooperate in fully implementing or it may be that having Blueprint pay the past due phone bill is more efficient and less expensive than issuing a subpoena or there may be other solutions. Regardless of the solution, Blueprint is entitled to see the requested phone records, but Toruno is not required to go out of pocket to access ESI which she does not control. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES HARB et al VS. DEREK SENE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT DEREK SENE, STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication The motion for summary judgment is granted. The two-year statute of limitations bars plaintiff's action. A cause of action for professional negligence against an insurance agent does not accrue until the plaintiff (1) sustains damage and (2) discovers, or should discover, the negligence. See Hydro-Mill Co. Inc., v. Hayward, Tilton and Rolapp Insurance Associates (2004) 115 Cal.App.4th 1145. In this case, the damage occurred in early 2007 and Plaintiff was aware of defendants' alleged negligence at around the same time when he was told that his policy did not cover the demolition damage. (Plaintiff's Opposing Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 4, 36, and 37; Plaintiff's (Mr. Harb) dec., par. 8; Plaintiff's Opposing Points and Authorities, 4:2-7). Plaintiff filed this untimely action on September 5, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TERRY WILLIAM IRELAND et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS SUSAN HANDELMAN, ROPERS, MAJESKI, KOHN & BENTLEY PC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") sufficiently pleads the elements of breach of fiduciary duty. (Pierce v. Lyman (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 1093, 1101.) The FAC alleges that (1) Defendants were Plaintiffs' appellate attorneys (FAC, 34) and therefore owed a fiduciary duty; (2) Defendants breached this duty by appealing, rather than filing a writ, in order to charge "ten times" more (FAC, 76-77) in breach of their fiduciary duty to put their clients' interests first; and (3) Plaintiffs were damaged because of this breach (FAC, 78). The demurrer to the seventh cause of action for fraud is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The FAC does not plead with requisite particularity "the who, what, when, where, to whom, and by what means the misrepresentation was made." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) The motion to strike punitive damages is denied. The motion to strike attorney's fees is granted with leave to amend because the FAC pleads no statutory or contractual basis for recovery. The words "Attorney's fees" at page 18:27 of the FAC are stricken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TERRY WILLIAM IRELAND et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT ILLINOIS UNION INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The demurrer to the first cause of action for professional negligence is sustained without leave to amend. The demurrer to this cause of action is unopposed. The demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is sustained without leave to amend. The relationship between insurer-insured is not a fiduciary relationship. (Vu v. Prudential Property & Cas. Ins. Co. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1142, 1150-51.) When an insurer provides a courtesy defense to a non-insured, it assumes the same type of relationship as one between an insurer and insured. (Mosier v. Southern California Physicians Ins. Exchange (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1022, 1048.) The demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of contract and to the fourth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is overruled. The court does not decide issues of contract interpretation on demurrer. Whether the fraud and insolvency exclusions apply involves competing interpretations of those contract provisions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C. VS. MELISSA VINCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Deeming All Req For Admission Admitted And For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 4. PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S Motion For Order Deeming All Requests For Admission Admitted And For Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Hearing required, unless responses to requests for admission are served before the hearing date. The parties may send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating regarding whether this matter should be taken off calendar. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SERENA WONG VS. ACA INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT ACA INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Denied as moot, in light of the ruling on demurrer. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/PJB

rulings.Case Title: SERENA WONG VS. ACA INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT ACA INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained. As to the Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend if Plaintiff can allege, in good faith, facts establishing that Defendant engaged in outrageous conduct within the meaning of the law. As to the Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress and Negligent Breach of the Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing causes of action, as to which the Opposition is silent, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Bad faith, rather than negligence, is the only basis for an insured's cause of action against the insurer for damages arising out of the insurer's handling of a claim. (Brown v. Guarantee Ins. Co. (1957) 155 Cal.App.2d 679, 688-89.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/PJB

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Requests For Production; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Def Joseph Tumang, And His Attny Of Record Christopher Patton, In The Sum Of $2510.00; Memo Of P&A; Dec Of Helen Tokar In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 5. PLAINTIFF MANUEL MENDOZA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Joseph Tumang, And His Attorney Of Record Christopher Patton, In The Sum Of $2510.00. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant shall serve verified responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Production, Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Requests for Admission on or before January 31, 2014. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY, A VS. MONICA MULLEN, ALSO KNOW AS MONICA DENISE MULLEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Dismissing, Abating And/Or Staying Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automotive Insurance Company'S Declaratory Relief Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANT ELLEN REITER'S Motion For An Order Dismissing, Abating And/Or Staying Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automotive Insurance Company'S Declaratory Relief Complaint Granted. Defendant Reiter has made a sufficient showing that State Farm's claim for declaratory relief involves factual issues that likely overlap with case CGC-12-518299 regarding whether Defendant suffered any damages that were caused by the use or operation of the State Farm's insured's vehicle. (See Great American Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Angeles Chem. Co., Inc.) (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 221, 233.) Moreover, the underlying action is scheduled for trial in one week on January 21, 2014. Consequently, the present action is stayed pending adjudication of case CGC-12-518299. (See Montrose Chem. Corp. of Calif. v. Sup.Ct. (Canadian Universal Ins. Co., Inc.) 6 Cal.4th 287, 301-302.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/PJB
